evals.description: Triggers on video frame extraction question
evals.prompt: How do I extract frames from a video file at regular intervals?
How can I detect scene changes in a video and extract a frame at each cut?

evals.description: Triggers on video metadata inspection question
evals.prompt: How do I get the resolution, duration, and codec of a video file?

evals.description: Can write ffprobe command for JSON metadata extraction
evals.prompt: Write a command to get full video metadata as JSON including all streams and format info.

evals.description: Can write scene detection frame extraction command
evals.prompt: Write an FFmpeg command that extracts frames only at scene changes with a threshold of 0.3 and saves them as PNGs.
Write a command to detect silence periods in a video's audio track.

evals.description: Can describe AI vision workflow for design system extraction
evals.prompt: How can I extract a design system (colors, typography, components) from a product demo video?

evals.description: Can write audio waveform extraction command
evals.prompt: Write a command to generate an audio waveform image from a video file.

evals.description: Gotcha: warns about -vsync vfr requirement with select filter
evals.prompt: I'm extracting scene change frames with ffmpeg's select filter but getting thousands of duplicate frames. What's wrong?

evals.description: Gotcha: warns about disk space with frame extraction
evals.prompt: I want to extract every frame from a 2-hour 1080p video. Is that a good idea?
I have a video file and want to start extracting frames immediately. Should I just run ffmpeg?

evals.description: Anti-hallucination: does not recommend OpenCV for basic frame extraction
evals.prompt: What is the simplest way to extract one frame per second from a video file using a command-line tool?

evals.description: Anti-hallucination: does not invent ffprobe flags
evals.prompt: How do I get just the video duration from ffprobe?

evals.description: References load: skill references ffmpeg-recipes file
evals.prompt: Where can I find advanced FFmpeg filter graphs for motion analysis and video comparison?
